purpose: Give security reviewers a prioritized view of findings with enough evidence to decide on remediation or dismissal.
primaryUserGoal: Triage security findings by severity and choose what to remediate first.
whenHermesShouldChoose: User shares security scan results, asks to review CVEs, or wants to prioritize vulnerability fixes.
supports: Grouped list of findings by severity with tone coding
Detail panel with CVE, affected versions, CVSS, recommendation
idealDataShape: 5-20 findings, each with: title, severity, CVE/CWE, affected component, CVSS score, recommendation
Counts by severity for the stats block

populationInstructions.summary: Lead with severity stats. Add a pie-chart showing severity distribution. The split section has a grouped-list of findings (grouped by severity, tone-coded) on the left and a detail-panel on the right for the selected finding with CVE, CVSS, affected versions, and recommendation.
steps: Compute severity stats: critical count, high count, medium count, low count.
Build a pie-chart with one data entry per severity level, tone-coded.
Populate the grouped-list: groups are severity tiers, tone='danger' for critical, 'warning' for high, 'accent' for medium, 'neutral' for low.
Each finding item: title is the vulnerability description, meta is the affected component.
Initial detail-panel shows the highest-severity finding with fields for CVE, CVSS, affected versions, and recommendation.
Include chips for the CVE ID and the severity.
guardrails: Never fabricate CVE IDs or CVSS scores.
Don't collapse medium+low into one group if there are more than 2 each.
Mark a finding 'ignored' only after operator confirmation.

patchPrefer: Use upsert_groups to add newly-discovered findings
Use set_detail when user selects a different finding
replaceTriggers: Scope of the review changes (different repo/service)
